title: ophioglossum vulgatum var. taylorianum
intro:
Ophioglossum vulgatum var. taylorianum, also known as Taylor's Adder's Tongue, is a rare and endangered variety of the Adder's Tongue fern, a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae.

This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Taylor's Adder's Tongue include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use is often limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
